input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-25T00:50:43.974Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) and is hitting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference — MTP returns a non-zero exit code when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated that as success.

Let me look at their project files to give specific guidance.

data.content:
This is a known MTP behavioral difference. **Exit code 8 means "no tests discovered"** — MTP treats zero tests as an error, while VSTest silently returned 0.

**Two ways to fix it:**

### Option 1 — CI command-line fix (no code change)
Pass `--minimum-expected-tests 0` through to the MTP runner:

```bash
dotnet test -- --minimum-expected-tests 0
```

### Option 2 — Project-level fix (recommended)
This keeps the fix with the project so every CI/dev environment picks it up automatically. Add the setting to your `.csproj`:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow zero tests so conditional-compilation configs don't fail CI -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With MTP, when no tests are discovered (because all tests are behind `#if INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` and that symbol isn't defined), MTP returns exit code 8 ("No tests were discovered"). VSTest returned 0 in this case.

The fix is to set the `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE` environment variable or use `--ignore-exit-code` flag, or more specifically for this scenario, you can set the `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` MSBuild property.

Let me check the specific MSBuild property name. Actually for MTP, the proper way to handle this is:


- Exit code 8 = "No tests found" in MTP
- You can use `--ignore-exit-code 8` on the command line
- Or set env var `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE=8`

But there might also be a more specific approach. For `dotnet test` with MTP, you can pass `-- --ignore-exit-code 8` to route it through to the test runner, or use `--minimum-expected-tests 0` to tell MTP that zero tests is acceptable. The cleanest solution would be an MSBuild property, but there's no direct property for this in the project file—the recommended approaches are CLI-based with either the ignore exit code flag or the minimum expected tests option. For a project-level fix, I can configure MTP through a `.testingplatformconfig.json` file or runsettings, which would be cleaner than requiring CI pipeline changes. The semantic approach of using `--minimum-expected-tests 0` is better than ignoring exit code 8, since it explicitly declares the expectation rather than masking the error.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow zero tests so conditional-compilation configs don't fail CI -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText: Hmm, it seems like `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` might not be the right MSBuild property for MSTest 3.8.0. Let me check if there's a different approach. Let me look at what's in the test results log.
OK so the `--minimum-expected-tests 0` IS being passed through. But it still failed. Let me check higher up in the log for the actual error.

data.content: The `--minimum-expected-tests` requires a non-zero value in this version. The correct fix is `--ignore-exit-code 8`:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests so conditional-compilation configs don't fail CI -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests so conditional-compilation configs don't fail CI (exit code 8 = no tests discovered) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
The error is clear: `--minimum-expected-tests` expects a **non-zero positive integer** in MSTest 3.8.0. So `0` is not valid.

The correct approach is to use `--ignore-exit-code 8` instead. Exit code 8 is the "no tests discovered" code.

Let me update the project file.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests so conditional-compilation configs don't fail CI -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests so conditional-compilation configs don't fail CI (exit code 8 = no tests discovered) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

toolArgs.old_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests so conditional-compilation configs don't fail CI -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
toolArgs.new_str:
<!-- Allow zero tests so conditional-compilation configs don't fail CI (exit code 8 = no tests discovered) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**Fixed.** The root cause: MTP returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered, unlike VSTest which returned 0.

The fix is one line in your `.csproj`:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

This tells the MTP runner to return exit code 0 instead of 8 when no tests are found. It's embedded in the project file so it applies everywhere (CI, local, IDE) without requiring pipeline changes. Real test failures still produce non-zero exit codes.

**Alternative** (if you prefer a CI-only fix):
```bash
dotnet test -- --ignore-exit-code 8
```
